shows.title: The Lion King
shows.description: Disney's award-winning musical continues to dazzle after 26 spectacular years, bringing the African savanna to life with stunning puppetry and a soaring score.

shows.title: Wicked
shows.description: The untold story of the witches of Oz remains one of London's most popular musicals, exploring an unlikely friendship before Dorothy arrives.

shows.title: Hamilton
shows.description: Lin-Manuel Miranda's Pulitzer and Tony-winning phenomenon mixes hip-hop, R&B, and classic theatre to tell the story of Alexander Hamilton.

shows.title: Phantom of the Opera
shows.description: Andrew Lloyd Webber's gothic romance returns — the masked Phantom haunts the Paris Opera House in pursuit of his obsession with a young soprano.

shows.title: Les Misérables
shows.description: The legendary adaptation of Victor Hugo's masterpiece featuring iconic songs and the enduring story of revolution, redemption and love.

shows.title: Paddington The Musical
shows.description: World premiere of the beloved bear's musical debut. Received 11 Olivier Award nominations — the most of any show this season.

shows.title: Beetlejuice
shows.description: The long-awaited London premiere of the Broadway hit. Meet Lydia Deetz, a teenager obsessed with a demon ready to unleash mayhem, featuring spectacular special effects.

shows.title: Avenue Q
shows.description: The multi-Tony Award-winning puppet-filled musical comedy returns to the West End for the first time since 2010, featuring irreverent humour and showstoppers.

shows.title: Kinky Boots
shows.description: The joyful West End revival starring Strictly's Johannes Radebe, featuring Cyndi Lauper's music and the story of an unlikely partnership between a shoemaker and a drag queen.

shows.title: Back to the Future: The Musical
shows.description: The award-winning stage adaptation where the iconic DeLorean flies across the stage. Final performances before it leaves the West End.

shows.title: Moulin Rouge! The Musical
shows.description: A spectacular celebration of truth, beauty, freedom, and love set in the legendary Parisian nightclub, with a pop soundtrack mashup.

shows.title: Starlight Express
shows.description: Andrew Lloyd Webber's roller-skating musical returns to London for the first time in 40 years with high-energy performances and thrilling stunts.

shows.title: Hadestown
shows.description: The mythical musical continues to enchant with a fresh cast including Olivier Award-winning Clive Rowe as Hermes, weaving the tale of Orpheus and Eurydice.

shows.title: Operation Mincemeat
shows.description: The 2024 Olivier Award-winning Best New Musical with 88 five-star reviews, the best-reviewed show in West End history. Now also a Tony winner.

shows.title: Oliver!
shows.description: Cameron Mackintosh's Olivier Award-winning staging of Lionel Bart's classic, with all-new choreography by Matthew Bourne. Timeless songs and Victorian London brought to life.

shows.title: Six
shows.description: Henry VIII's six wives take the mic and tell their stories in a dazzling, high-energy pop concert musical by Toby Marlow and Lucy Moss.

shows.title: The Devil Wears Prada
shows.description: The hit film becomes a West End musical, with songs by Sir Elton John. Follow Andy Sachs into the world of high fashion under the fearsome Miranda Priestly.

shows.title: The Book of Mormon
shows.description: The irreverent musical comedy from the creators of South Park follows two mismatched Mormon missionaries sent to Uganda. Winner of nine Tony Awards.

shows.title: A Mirrored Monet
shows.description: Brand-new musical reimagining Monet's attempts to paint The Water Lilies in 1916. Jeff Shankley and Dean John-Wilson star as Monet at different points in his life.

shows.title: Dracula
shows.description: Three-time Oscar nominee Cynthia Erivo stars as all 23 roles in Kip Williams' acclaimed reimagining of Bram Stoker's gothic horror — a one-woman tour de force.

shows.title: Oh, Mary!
shows.description: Cole Escola's Tony-winning dark comedy starring Mason Alexander Park as Mary Todd Lincoln. Gleefully outrageous, packed with gags and mayhem.

shows.title: Inter Alia
shows.description: Rosamund Pike stars in the world premiere of Suzie Miller's searing exploration of modern masculinity and motherhood, from the Prima Facie team.

shows.title: Romeo & Juliet
shows.description: Robert Icke directs Sadie Sink and Noah Jupe in their West End debuts in Shakespeare's timeless tale of forbidden love.

shows.title: Harry Potter and the Cursed Child
shows.description: The eighth Harry Potter story told in a spectacular stage production featuring breathtaking theatrical magic and spellbinding effects.

shows.title: The Mousetrap
shows.description: Agatha Christie's legendary whodunnit — the longest-running show in the world. Marking its 75th anniversary with new director Ola Ince.

shows.title: My Neighbour Totoro
shows.description: The RSC's enchanting stage adaptation of Studio Ghibli's beloved animated film transfers to the West End after a triumphant Barbican run.

shows.title: Teeth 'n' Smiles
shows.description: Rebecca Lucy Taylor (Self Esteem) stars in the 50th-anniversary revival of David Hare's gritty look at the collapse of the 1960s rock dream.

shows.title: The Play That Goes Wrong
shows.description: The Olivier Award-winning comedy about a drama society's disastrous attempt to stage a 1920s murder mystery, where everything spectacularly falls apart.

shows.title: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest
shows.description: The first major London revival in over 20 years. Clint Dyer directs Aaron Pierre and Giles Terera as two inmates in a hellish psychiatric ward.

shows.title: Broken Glass
shows.description: Arthur Miller's rarely performed Olivier Award-winning drama gets a searing new revival. In 1938 Brooklyn, a Jewish woman has an unexpected reaction to Nazi atrocities.

shows.title: A Doll's House
shows.description: Romola Garai takes on the monumental role of Nora in Ibsen's explosive proto-feminist classic, in a modern adaptation by Anya Reiss.

shows.title: Evening All Afternoon
shows.description: Erin Kellyman (28 Years Later) makes her stage debut alongside Anastasia Hille in Anna Ziegler's moving story about overcoming grief.

shows.title: Marie & Rosetta
shows.description: Beverley Knight stars in this joyful tribute to pioneering gospel singer Sister Rosetta Tharpe, transporting audiences to 1940s Mississippi.

shows.title: Choir Boy
shows.description: Academy Award-winner Tarell Alvin McCraney's drama about class, sexuality and singing at an elite all-Black prep school. Directed by Nancy Medina.

shows.title: Manic Street Creature
shows.description: Multi-award-winning musical returning for a brand-new limited run. Maimuna Memon's Edinburgh Fringe hit about a driven musician becoming divorced from reality.

shows.title: Jason Manford: A Manford All Seasons
shows.description: The beloved comedian brings his latest stand-up tour to Soho Theatre's newest venue in Walthamstow.

shows.title: Murder, She Didn't Write
shows.description: An ingenious improvised comedy inspired by Agatha Christie. A huge Edinburgh Fringe success — the audience helps create a new murder mystery every night.

shows.title: Mayerling
shows.description: Kenneth MacMillan's psychologically thrilling ballet about a real scandal in the Austro-Hungarian court. Dark desires, family secrets and political intrigue.

shows.title: Crystal Pite & Kameron N Saunders
shows.description: A one-of-a-kind evening blending dance, spoken word, and live orchestral score by ENB Philharmonic, exploring the complexities of human existence.

shows.title: ABBA Voyage
shows.venue: ABBA Arena, Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park
shows.description: A ground-breaking concert featuring digital ABBA avatars performing hits with a live band in a purpose-built arena.

shows.title: Chat Noir
shows.description: Daring fire acts and spellbinding artistry at this immersive cabaret experience in a secret London venue. Feel the heat of the night.

shows.title: Jeff Wayne's War of the Worlds
shows.description: Live actors, VR technology, and immersive surround sound bring the Martian invasion to terrifying life in this long-running immersive experience.

shows.title: Les Liaisons Dangereuses
shows.description: Lesley Manville and Aidan Turner star in the first National Theatre staging of Christopher Hampton's acclaimed adaptation, directed by Marianne Elliott.

shows.title: Pride The Musical
shows.description: Based on the acclaimed 2014 film. The story of the solidarity the British LGBT community offered striking miners in the '80s, directed by Matthew Warchus.

shows.title: The Last Five Years
shows.description: 25th anniversary concert starring Rachel Zegler and Ben Platt in this emotional portrayal of two New Yorkers falling in and out of love.

shows.title: Faulty Towers The Dining Experience
shows.description: The world-famous interactive dining experience based on the classic BBC sitcom. Two hours of chaos, slapstick and a three-course meal.

shows.title: Unfortunate: The Untold Story of Ursula the Sea Witch
shows.description: Fresh from Edinburgh Fringe — a sassy, salacious, campy retelling of The Little Mermaid from the villain's perspective. Pop songs, innuendos and glitter.
